name: GridPoint Energy Management API
what_it_does: GridPoint energy management REST API for commercial building energy optimization, demand response, and IoT energy control. Enables AI agents to manage HVAC, lighting, and plug load controls for building energy optimization, handle demand response event participation and load curtailment automation for utility program compliance, access real-time energy consumption monitoring and anomaly detection for energy management, retrieve equipment performance analytics and fault detection for building systems diagnostics, manage multi-site energy portfolio monitoring for enterprise energy management, handle utility rate optimization and time-of-use scheduling for energy cost reduction, access carbon emission tracking and sustainability reporting for ESG compliance, retrieve energy baseline calculation and M&V data for energy savings verification, manage demand charge reduction strategies for commercial electricity cost control, and integrate GridPoint with BMS, utility demand response platforms, and sustainability reporting systems.
use_cases: Controlling building HVAC from energy optimization agents
Responding to demand response events from utility program agents
Monitoring energy anomalies from building diagnostics agents
Reporting carbon emissions from sustainability agents
not_for: Residential energy management without commercial scale
Industrial process energy without building systems focus
Utility-scale generation without end-use customer context
best_when: A multi-site commercial real estate operator, retail chain, or enterprise facility manager wanting AI agents to automate energy optimization, demand response participation, fault detection, and sustainability reporting across building portfolios.
avoid_when: DEMAND RESPONSE CURTAILMENT RISK: Automated demand response load curtailment must account for occupant comfort and critical business operations; automated HVAC setback during demand response events without override capability creates tenant comfort complaints and lease violation risk. Building systems control safety — automated HVAC and lighting control must respect safety setpoints and fire alarm integration; automated building control without hardware safety override creates occupant safety risk. Utility demand response contractual obligation — automated demand response participation creates contractual curtailment obligations with utility; automated systems that fail to curtail per event notification create utility penalty exposure. M&V methodology accuracy — automated energy savings verification must follow IPMVP Option A/B/C methodology; automated baseline calculations with incorrect normalization create energy savings reporting inaccuracy for financing and incentive programs.

auth.notes: GridPoint uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. Arlington, Virginia HQ. Founded 2003. Private (Battery Ventures, Kleiner Perkins alumni investors). Energy management hardware and software platform. 50,000+ commercial sites managed. Demand response aggregator for PJM, CAISO, and other ISOs. Competes with EnerNOC (acquired by Enel), Echelon, and Enphase for commercial building energy management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — hardware deployment required plus software subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual software subscription; hardware cost for IoT energy sensors and controls
pricing.notes: Arlington, Virginia. Private. Founded 2003. 50,000+ commercial sites. Hardware + software platform. Demand response aggregator.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; real-time control commands have strict SLA

known_agent_gotchas: DEMAND RESPONSE CURTAILMENT SAFETY: Automated DR load curtailment must implement occupant override and critical load protection; automated HVAC setback without safeguards creates tenant comfort violation and potential safety risk in extreme weather

Utility DR contractual obligations — automated demand response non-compliance (failing to curtail per event) creates utility penalty; ensure curtailment automation has reliable execution path and failover

Building systems safety override — automated HVAC and lighting control must respect fire alarm, smoke control, and emergency systems integration; building automation without hardware safety interlock creates life safety risk

IPMVP M&V accuracy — automated energy savings baseline and measurement must follow recognized M&V methodology; incorrect weather normalization or baseline period selection creates energy performance contract (EPC) payment dispute risk
Hardware dependency — GridPoint energy management requires GridPoint IoT hardware deployment; API-only integrations without hardware have limited real-time control capability
ISO demand response rules — demand response event participation rules vary by ISO (PJM, CAISO, ERCOT, MISO); automated DR agents must apply correct curtailment protocols per ISO program enrollment
agent_readiness.security_notes: Building energy management. SOC2. OAuth2 with scopes. US. IoT energy control and building systems data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Building energy IoT. SOC2. OAuth2 with scopes. US. Energy control and building data.
